title: Data Analysis Challenge
description: Analyze real datasets and make data-driven decisions

tasks.task: You have sales data over 12 months. Which chart best shows trends over time?
tasks.explanation: Line charts are perfect for showing trends and changes over time, making them ideal for monthly sales data.

tasks.task: You find that ice cream sales and drowning incidents both increase in summer. What's the relationship?
tasks.scenario: Data shows: Ice cream sales ↑, Temperature ↑, Drowning incidents ↑
choices: Both are caused by hot weather (confounding variable)
tasks.explanation: This is a classic example of correlation vs causation. Hot weather causes both increased ice cream sales and more swimming (leading to more drowning incidents).

tasks.task: In this dataset of student ages: [18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 45], what should you do with the value 45?
tasks.explanation: Outliers should be investigated, not automatically removed. It could be a mature student, data entry error, or important edge case.

tasks.task: You want to know if a new teaching method improved test scores. Which test should you use?
tasks.scenario: Before method: Mean=75, n=30. After method: Mean=82, n=30. Same students.
tasks.explanation: A paired t-test is perfect for comparing the same group's performance before and after an intervention.

tasks.task: Your model has 95% accuracy but terrible performance on minority class. What's the issue?
tasks.scenario: Dataset: 95% class A, 5% class B. Model predicts class A every time.
tasks.explanation: With severe class imbalance, a model can achieve high accuracy by always predicting the majority class. Use balanced accuracy, F1-score, or resampling techniques.
